name: Woebot Health
desc: Pioneer AI-based CBT chatbot for emotional support, anxiety, and depression. Clinician-designed and FDA Breakthrough Device designated.

name: Wysa
desc: AI-powered mental health support app using evidence-based techniques. Serves consumers and enterprise with human therapist escalation path.

name: Replika
desc: AI companion for emotional connection and social support. Controversial for romantic/friendship personas; large user base with significant organized advocacy community.

name: Youper
desc: AI-powered emotional health assistant using CBT and ACT techniques. Tracks mood, guides self-reflection, and connects to therapists.

name: Headspace
desc: Mindfulness and meditation app integrating AI personalization. One of the largest mental wellness apps globally; partners with healthcare systems and employers.

name: Calm
desc: Sleep, meditation, and relaxation app integrating AI for personalized content. Partners with healthcare systems and employers.

name: Earkick
desc: Real-time AI mental health companion for anxiety management; no account required. Emphasizes privacy-first design.

name: Koko (Noni)
desc: AI-assisted peer support platform blending human and AI responses for emotional support at scale. Originated as an MIT academic research project.

name: Pi (by Inflection AI)
desc: Conversational AI positioned as a personal assistant and emotional support companion. Subsequently acquired by Microsoft.

name: Character.AI
desc: Platform for AI character personas. Widely used by teens for companionship; under significant scrutiny for mental health risks to youth.

name: Spring Health
desc: Precision mental healthcare platform using AI to match users to care pathways. Covers therapy, coaching, and medication management.

name: Talkspace
desc: Large telehealth therapy platform integrating AI for therapist matching, documentation, and care navigation.

name: BetterHelp
desc: Large online therapy platform integrating AI tools for therapist matching and messaging support.

name: Limbic
desc: AI-powered mental health triage and assessment used by NHS in the UK. Automates referrals into talking therapies (IAPT/TALKIAD).

name: Eleos Health
desc: AI ambient documentation tool for behavioral health clinicians. Reduces administrative burden through automated session notes.

name: Nabla
desc: AI copilot for clinicians: ambient note-taking, summarization, and care coordination for mental and physical health.

name: Lyra Health
desc: Employer-facing mental health platform using AI-driven measurement-based care. Covers therapy, coaching, and medication.

name: Quartet Health
desc: AI platform integrating behavioral and physical health, facilitating care coordination between providers and payers.

name: Osmind
desc: EHR and outcomes platform for ketamine, TMS, and other innovative psychiatric treatments. Uses AI for outcomes tracking.

name: DeepScribe
desc: Ambient AI medical scribe for behavioral health and primary care. Captures and structures clinical conversations automatically.

name: Innerworld
desc: Metaverse-based peer support and therapy platform using avatars for anonymity, targeting anxiety and depression.

name: Epic Systems
desc: Dominant EHR vendor integrating AI suicide risk scoring, depression screening (PHQ-9 automation), and behavioral health workflows into hospital systems nationwide.

name: Stanford HAI
desc: Stanford's Human-Centered AI Institute funds and conducts research on AI in mental health, including clinical efficacy and governance ethics.

name: MIT Media Lab — Affective Computing
desc: Rosalind Picard's group pioneered affective computing — the science of machines that recognize and respond to human emotions. Foundational to the field.

name: CMU Language Technologies Institute
desc: Research on NLP for mental health signal detection in speech and text, including depression and suicide risk assessment models.

name: USC Institute for Creative Technologies
desc: Develops Ellie, a virtual human interviewer for detecting depression and PTSD in veterans. Pioneering work in avatar-based mental health assessment.

name: Oxford Internet Institute
desc: Research on digital mental health, screen time, social media harms, and AI impacts on adolescent wellbeing. Highly influential in policy debates.

name: Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health
desc: Research programs studying AI, social media, and population mental health outcomes from a public health epidemiology lens.

name: NIMH
desc: National Institute of Mental Health funds research on AI biomarkers, precision psychiatry, and digital phenotyping for mental health conditions.

name: Alan Turing Institute
desc: UK's national AI institute conducting research on responsible AI in healthcare including mental health applications and governance frameworks.

name: Brown University ARIA
desc: Brown's AI Research Institute on Interaction for AI Assistants researches human-AI interaction including conversational agents in therapeutic and support contexts.

name: University of Washington (HCDE)
desc: UW Human Centered Design & Engineering research on accessible mental health technology design, community-participatory approaches, and human-centered AI.

name: Human Line Project
desc: Project exploring the boundary between human and AI-delivered mental health support, including research on user trust, safety, and outcomes.

name: NIST AI Safety Institute
desc: Develops AI Risk Management Frameworks (AI RMF) applied to high-stakes health AI. Increasingly relevant to mental health AI safety standards.

name: Data & Society Research Institute
desc: Researches social and cultural implications of data-centric technologies including mental health data practices, surveillance, crisis tech, and AI care systems. Produces influential investigative reports.

name: Center for Humane Technology
desc: Advocates for technology design that protects human wellbeing; focused on social media mental health harms, AI risks, and broad public education campaigns.

name: Mental Health America
desc: National nonprofit championing mental health; engaged on digital mental health tools, equitable access, and technology standards for the field.

name: NAMI
desc: National Alliance on Mental Illness — largest grassroots mental health org in the US; engaged on AI-assisted peer support and crisis line technology.

name: Crisis Text Line
desc: Crisis support via text using AI and NLP to triage and route conversations. Controversy over data-sharing practices highlighted field-wide ethics gaps.

name: Trevor Project
desc: LGBTQ+ youth crisis intervention; developed its own AI model trained on conversations to extend counselor capacity and reduce response times.

name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
desc: US national crisis line integrating technology and AI-assisted routing. Federally funded, locally operated network replacing the old 1-800 number.

name: American Foundation for Suicide Prevention
desc: Funds research and advocates for safe messaging on AI platforms; works with tech companies on suicide prevention guidelines and content moderation.

name: Fairplay
desc: Formerly Campaign for a Commercial-Free Childhood. Advocates against exploitative commercial practices targeting children, including AI apps that may harm youth mental health.

name: Hearing Voices Network
desc: International network of people who hear voices and experience unusual perceptions; a critical voice on AI tools that may mischaracterize lived psychiatric experience.

name: SAMHSA
desc: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration sets standards for digital mental health tools and funds technology pilots nationally.

name: FDA Digital Health Center of Excellence
desc: Oversees Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) including AI mental health apps. Provides guidance on clinical AI tool approval pathways and regulatory frameworks.

name: FTC
desc: Federal Trade Commission monitors deceptive AI claims in mental health apps, consumer data monetization, and unfair commercial practices in digital health.

name: European Commission (EU AI Act)
desc: The EU AI Act classifies AI for mental health and wellbeing under high-risk or limited-risk categories requiring transparency, oversight, and mandatory human review.

name: WHO (Digital Mental Health)
desc: WHO has issued guidance on digital mental health interventions, chatbot ethics, and AI integration into global mental health systems and crisis response.

name: NCSL
desc: National Conference of State Legislatures provides research and analysis to US state legislatures on AI in health, mental health app regulation, and data privacy bills.

name: Coalition of 42 Attorneys General
desc: Bipartisan coalition of state AGs investigating and litigating against social media platforms and AI products for youth mental health harms and deceptive design.

name: CDT (Center for Democracy & Technology)
desc: Conducts policy research and advocacy on civil rights in AI, mental health data privacy, AI discrimination in healthcare, and platform accountability.

name: OECD AI in Health
desc: Publishes policy guidance on AI in healthcare including mental health AI governance, safety standards, and equity frameworks across member countries.

name: Bipartisan Policy Center
desc: Works on mental health policy including telehealth, digital tools, and AI integration into behavioral health systems across partisan lines.

name: PAI (Partnership on AI)
desc: Multi-stakeholder org bringing together civil society, academia, and industry to develop responsible AI practices; runs working groups on AI in sensitive sectors including mental health.

name: Andreessen Horowitz (a16z Bio)
desc: a16z Bio fund has backed multiple AI mental health startups. Significant thought leadership shaping the digital health startup ecosystem.

name: General Catalyst
desc: Major investor in mental health tech including Spring Health and Lyra. Health Assurance thesis focuses on preventive, AI-driven care at population scale.

name: Bessemer Venture Partners
desc: Active mental health tech investor with portfolio across multiple AI-assisted behavioral health platforms.

name: Google Ventures (GV)
desc: GV portfolio includes mental health apps and AI health tools. Google integrates mental health resources and crisis information into Search and Maps at scale.

name: Sequoia Capital
desc: Active digital health investor with mental health portfolio companies; shapes startup ecosystem direction and capital formation for the space.

name: Electronic Frontier Foundation (EFF)
desc: Advocates for privacy and civil liberties in AI; critical watchdog on mental health data exploitation by apps, insurers, and AI platforms.

name: AI Now Institute
desc: Researches social implications of AI including mental health surveillance, algorithmic bias in healthcare, labor displacement, and corporate power.

name: Algorithmic Justice League
desc: Highlights harms of biased AI systems; directly relevant to mental health AI that may disadvantage or misdiagnose marginalized communities.

name: Access Now
desc: International digital rights organization monitoring AI mental health tools for privacy violations and discriminatory design across global contexts.

name: Center for AI Safety
desc: Advocates for AI safety broadly; increasingly relevant as AI mental health tools are deployed at scale with vulnerable populations.

name: EPIC (Electronic Privacy Information Center)
desc: DC-based privacy advocacy nonprofit focused on AI, data protection, and consumer rights. Active on mental health app data practices and health privacy law.

name: Consumer Federation of America
desc: Broad-based consumer advocacy organization; engaged on AI consumer protection including deceptive mental health app claims, data monetization, and digital safety.

name: Public Citizen
desc: Public interest advocacy group active on AI healthcare regulation, pharmaceutical industry accountability, and consumer protection in digital health markets.

name: Youth for America
desc: Youth-centered advocacy organization working on digital safety, social media harms, and technology policy affecting young people's mental health and development.

name: Big Brother Watch
desc: UK civil liberties org tracking AI surveillance in healthcare and mental health contexts, particularly NHS AI deployments and automated decision-making.

name: American Psychiatric Association
desc: Publishes guidance for psychiatrists on AI tools, digital mental health, telehealth, and ethical use of technology in clinical practice.

name: American Psychological Association
desc: Sets ethical standards for psychologists using AI tools; monitors AI-delivered therapy efficacy and professional competency questions in digital practice.

name: NASW (National Association of Social Workers)
desc: Developing ethical guidance on AI use in social work and mental health case management. Concerned with automation, equity, and displacement in social services.

name: AMIA (American Medical Informatics Association)
desc: Drives research and practice standards for AI in clinical settings including behavioral health informatics, EHR integration, and AI decision support.

name: International Society for Mental Health Online (ISMHO)
desc: Sets guidelines and practices for online mental health services including AI-augmented therapy, digital interventions, and e-therapy standards.

name: STAT News
desc: Authoritative health and science journalism covering AI mental health startups, clinical evidence debates, regulatory decisions, and policy developments.

name: MIT Technology Review
desc: In-depth technology journalism and analysis on AI mental health products, ethics, clinical evidence standards, and societal implications.

name: The Markup
desc: Investigative journalism on technology harms; has broken stories on mental health app data practices, ad targeting of vulnerable users, and exploitation.

name: Baratunde Thurston
desc: Writer, comedian, and technology culture commentator. Host of "How to Citizen" — covers tech's effects on democracy, mental health, and community.

name: Dr. Alfiee Breland-Noble (AAKOMA Project)
desc: Mental health advocate and researcher focused on mental health in communities of color; public commentator on AI equity in mental health tools and youth wellbeing.

name: The Verge
desc: Broad tech journalism outlet with consistent coverage of consumer mental health AI, chatbot controversies, platform harms, and teen safety debates.

name: Mad in America
desc: Platform for those critical of mainstream psychiatry; covers AI mental health tech through a survivor and critical psychiatry lens. Publishes research critiques and personal narratives.

name: Wellcome Trust
desc: One of the world's largest funders of mental health research; major investor in digital mental health, AI therapeutics, and global mental health innovation.

name: Robert Wood Johnson Foundation
desc: Largest US health-focused philanthropy; funds digital mental health equity, AI ethics in healthcare, and community-based technology programs.

name: MacArthur Foundation
desc: Funds criminal justice, mental health equity, and the intersection of AI and human wellbeing across systems of care.

name: One Mind
desc: Funds brain health research and accelerates mental health innovation, including AI-assisted diagnostics, biomarkers, and therapeutics.

name: Cohen Veterans Network
desc: Funds mental health care for veterans and military families; supports AI and digital innovation in veteran mental health services and PTSD treatment.

name: JPB Foundation
desc: Funds poverty reduction and health equity; supports community-level mental health programs integrating accessible technology for low-income communities.

name: Google DeepMind
desc: Research on AI models for mental health risk detection. Google integrates mental health resources into Search, YouTube, and Maps at massive population scale.

name: Microsoft Research
desc: Microsoft Research studies AI for mental health detection, emotional AI, and wellbeing. Azure powers many mental health AI tools; Teams integrates wellbeing features.

name: Meta
desc: Owns Instagram and Facebook — at the center of the youth mental health debate. Also developing AI companions and content moderation systems affecting billions of users.

name: Apple (Mental Health Features)
desc: Apple Health integrates mental health logging (mood, anxiety). Research studies on depression and anxiety conducted through Apple Watch partnerships with academic institutions.

name: OpenAI
desc: ChatGPT widely used as an unofficial mental health support tool. OpenAI collaborates with crisis organizations on safe messaging and is under growing scrutiny as de facto mental health infrastructure.

name: Salesforce Health Cloud
desc: Provides CRM for behavioral health providers; integrating AI for care coordination, patient engagement, and population health management.

name: r/replika (Reddit Community)
desc: Large online community of Replika AI companion users. Organized significant advocacy when Replika changed its features, demonstrating the political voice of AI companion user communities.

name: Youth Mental Health Advocates
desc: Loosely organized networks of young people (active on TikTok, Instagram, and Discord) sharing lived experiences with AI mental health tools and advocating for safer design and policy.

name: Peer Support Space
desc: Online community for mental health peer support; increasingly navigating how members use AI tools alongside human peer support networks.

name: NAMI Peer-to-Peer
desc: NAMI's peer education programs connect mental health peers in recovery; intersecting with questions about AI tool use in daily life and crisis situations.
